title: issue-type-field: add type to the record (bug/feature/epic/research/roadmap)
body:
# Goal

Add a first-class `type` field to every issue record.
Values: `bug`, `feature`, `epic`, `research`, `roadmap`,
`unspecified`. End state: every issue has a stable,
schema-level type marker that doesn't depend on label
discipline. `jjf new --type bug ...` sets it; `jjf ls
--type feature` filters by it; `jjf ready` (forthcoming)
sorts by type priority (bug > feature > research > epic
> unspecified, with roadmap excluded — it's not work
to do).

# Why now

`jjf ready` (`69d5e1b`) is sketched against `Bug` types
(blocked by `199ed91` Bug → Issue rename). Both tickets
need the type field to do their jobs correctly:

- `ready` needs to mechanize bugs-before-features: with
  no type marker, it can't tell defects from feature work.
- The rename (`199ed91`) is the natural carrier for
  schema bumps — we're already changing `bug_id` →
  `issue_id` in trailers. Adding a `type` field rides
  the same v1 → v2 wave. Doing it now means one spec
  bump, not two.

Per the discussion on 2026-06-22: label-by-convention
was rejected because label discipline erodes; mandating
a label on create was rejected for the same reason
(forgotten flags, no compiler help). First-class field
is the right shape because:

- Type is structural everywhere else: GitHub (added 2024),
  Gitea, Jira, Beads. Treating it as a label is a
  one-time convention; treating it as a field acknowledges
  what it actually is.
- `ready`'s priority logic depends on type being
  meaningful and stable. Schema-level beats convention.
- Future consumers (the planned MCP server, dashboards,
  external migrations from Beads) all expect type to be
  queryable.

# Sequencing

This ticket sits in a three-ticket chain:

1. `199ed91` (Bug → Issue rename, v1 → v2 spec bump). Lands
   first because it's the wider rename pass; this ticket
   adds one field on top of the renamed types.
2. **This ticket** (`type` field). Lands second, against
   the renamed types. Fold into the same v2 spec bump if
   `199ed91` hasn't shipped yet; otherwise rev to v2.1.
3. `69d5e1b` (`agent-ready`). Lands third, against the
   typed schema, so its priority sort has a real field
   to read.

# Scope

## Storage

- Add `IssueType` enum to `crates/jjf-storage/`:
  ```rust
  pub enum IssueType {
      Bug,
      Feature,
      Epic,
      Research,
      Roadmap,
      Unspecified,
  }
  ```
  Serde rename to lowercase; default = `Unspecified`.
- Add `type: IssueType` field to `IssueRecord` (or
  `BugRecord` if filed before `199ed91` lands). Place
  it after `status`, before `labels`.
- Add `Op::SetType { issue_id, kind: IssueType }` to
  the op enum. Set-once-with-create-then-mutable
  semantics — same as `set-status`.
- Add `IssueDraft.type_: Option<IssueType>` (default None
  → `Unspecified`).
- Extend `Storage::update(UpdateFields { type_:
  Option<Option<IssueType>>, ... })`. The double-Option
  follows the assignee pattern (None = leave alone;
  Some(...) = set).
- `Storage::create_issue` emits `Op::SetType` in the
  multi-op create commit when type is non-default
  (mirrors how `set-body` / `set-assignee` / `label-add`
  ops are conditionally emitted today per spec §5.7).

## Spec

- `docs/storage-format.md`:
  - §3 (`IssueRecord`): add `type` field with the
    enum values, default `unspecified`, position after
    `status`.
  - §5.2 (`Jjf-Op` vocabulary): add `set-type` op with
    payload `{ "issue_id": "...", "type": "..." }`.
  - §5.7 (multi-op create): list `set-type` among the
    ops the create commit may emit.
- This is a v2 spec change. If `199ed91` is shipping
  first as v2, fold into that. If shipping after,
  rev to v2.1.

## CLI

- `jjf new --type <bug|feature|epic|research|roadmap>`:
  optional flag. Default `unspecified`. Validates via
  clap `value_enum`.
- `jjf update <id> --type <kind>`: optional flag.
  Mirrors the multi-field update pattern.
- `jjf ls --type <kind>`: optional filter (repeatable
  via `--type bug --type feature` semantics: AND?
  probably OR since types are mutually exclusive).
- `jjf show <id>` and `jjf show --json` surface the
  field. Plain-text format: include a `type: bug`
  line near `status:` and `labels:`.
- All `--json` envelopes that surface the issue (new,
  show, ls, update) include `type` in the payload.

## Docs

- `docs/cli-json.md`: update every verb's example
  envelope where the issue record is shown. New
  `--type` flag documented on `new`, `update`, `ls`.
- `README.md`: the "What jjforge tracks" section
  mentions issues have a type — update to list the
  valid types.
- `CLAUDE.md`: brief note in "Creating a new issue"
  section recommending `--type` on every `jjf new`,
  with `feature` as the recommended default for new
  work and `bug` for defects.

## Migration

- Existing issues on the `bugs` bookmark (the 9 issues
  filed so far) have no `type` field. The read path
  defaults missing field to `Unspecified`. Operator
  can backfill in a one-time pass:
  ```bash
  jjf update 9566f52 --type roadmap
  jjf update <each-epic> --type epic
  jjf update <each-feature-ticket> --type feature
  jjf update <each-bug-ticket> --type bug
  ```
  Document this as a post-deploy step in the closing
  comment. Not strictly necessary (Unspecified is
  valid), but `ready` works better with the field
  populated.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `IssueType` enum + serde roundtrip test in
  `jjf-storage`.
- [ ] Field present in `IssueRecord`; integration test
  asserts byte-equality round-trip on a record with each
  type value.
- [ ] `Op::SetType` parses + replays correctly; history
  reader surfaces it.
- [ ] `Storage::update(... type_: Some(Some(IssueType::Bug)))`
  lands a `set-type` trailer; integration test verifies
  via `read_history`.
- [ ] `jjf new --type bug -t "x" -F -` creates an issue
  with `type=bug`; `jjf show` reports it.
- [ ] `jjf ls --type bug` filter works.
- [ ] `jjf update <id> --type feature` mutation works.
- [ ] `docs/storage-format.md` updated.
- [ ] `docs/cli-json.md` updated.
- [ ] `README.md` and `CLAUDE.md` updated.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green
  (currently 185).
- [ ] Migration recipe for existing issues recorded in
  the closing comment.

# Out of scope

- `jjf ready`'s type-priority sort: that's `69d5e1b`'s
  job, which depends on this.
- Renaming `Bug` → `Issue`: that's `199ed91`'s job. If
  this ticket files first, use the `Bug` types and let
  `199ed91` rename them along with everything else.
- Type-based dashboards, badge rendering, type-aware
  search. v1 surface is the field + filter.
- Subtypes (`bug:regression` vs `bug:new`, `feature:enhancement`
  vs `feature:new`). Labels can carry that nuance.
- Mandatory typing on create (forcing `--type` to be
  required). Default to `unspecified`; the operator
  picks when they care.

# Pointers

- Enum precedent: `Status` enum in
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/record.rs`.
- Op precedent: `Op::SetStatus` in
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/op.rs`.
- Multi-op create pattern: `Storage::create_issue`
  emits ops conditionally based on populated fields.
- `UpdateFields` and `Storage::update`: the existing
  double-Option pattern for "leave alone vs set vs
  unset" is the model.
- Spec §3.1 (current record shape), §5.2 (op vocabulary),
  §5.7 (multi-op create) all need edits.
- Dependent: `69d5e1b` (`agent-ready`) declares this as
  a prerequisite.
